region_of_origin: USA (Subway global product); widely available at Subway Singapore (~150 outlets)
food_subgroup: Fast food chicken sandwich; 6-inch sub on Italian white bread with rotisserie-style chicken breast pieces, standard vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cucumber), no sauce; ~220g per serving

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for a Subway Rotisserie-Style Chicken 6-inch sub. FOODON_03315674 (submarine sandwich) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for a Subway rotisserie chicken sub. A0GBY (fast food chicken sandwich) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for this specific commercial fast food item.

serving.description: 1 standard 6-inch sub (~220g, no sauce, standard vegetables)
serving.natural_language_portion: One 6-inch Subway Rotisserie-Style Chicken sub with standard vegetables (lettuce, tomato, cucumber) on Italian white bread and no sauce weighs approximately 220g and provides approximately 345 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Rotisserie-Style Chicken 6-inch — Subway Singapore Official Nutritional Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from Subway Singapore's official menu nutrition page for the Rotisserie-Style Chicken 6-inch sub with standard vegetables and no sauce. Per-100g values derived by dividing the declared per-serving values (220g serving) by 2.2. Subway Singapore is an approved branded food source under the USDA Branded Food / commercial product category. Micronutrient values (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, vitamins) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central entries for comparable chicken sub sandwiches and are retained from the verified input record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — white bread and soft wheat bread entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Rotisserie-Style Chicken 6-inch sub as a composite food. GI of 60 is estimated based on the Italian white bread component, which is the primary glycaemic driver. The University of Sydney GI Database records soft white wheat bread GI values in the range 55–75 (mean ~70 for standard white bread; lower for soft sub-style rolls). A GI of 60 is defensible for a soft Italian sub roll, consistent with published values for similar soft white wheat rolls in Atkinson et al. (2021). The chicken and vegetable components contribute negligible glycaemic load. GL per 220g serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 47.96) / 100 ≈ 28.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content: ~735mg per 6-inch serving (334mg per 100g), representing approximately 37% of the 2,000mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive individuals
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this sub without added sauces (each sauce adds 150–200mg sodium), choose it as an occasional meal rather than a daily option, and pair it with low-sodium foods for the remainder of the day to stay within the 2,000mg daily target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycaemic load of ~28.8 per serving driven by Italian white bread (GI ~60); 48g available carbohydrate per 6-inch sub requires careful portion and insulin management
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consider requesting wholegrain bread (GI ~55, +2g fibre) to reduce the glycaemic impact, omit or minimise sauces, and account for the full ~48g carbohydrate load in meal planning; the 23g protein from rotisserie chicken partially slows gastric emptying and attenuates the postprandial glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (~1.1g per serving, 0.5g per 100g) and zero trans fat; moderate sodium (735mg per serving) is the primary cardiovascular concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Subway Rotisserie-Style Chicken 6-inch sub (no sauce) is one of the lowest-saturated-fat fast food main meals available in Singapore and is acceptable for individuals with cardiovascular disease when consumed without high-sodium sauces, as part of a diet that otherwise limits sodium to under 2,000mg per day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.5g/100g); no deep-frying; vegetable toppings provide small amounts of anti-inflammatory vitamin C and quercetin
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The no-sauce rotisserie chicken sub is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its very low saturated fat content and absence of fried-fat oxidation products; moderate sodium (735mg) should be monitored if the individual is also managing hypertension as a comorbidity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Rotisserie-Style Chicken 6-inch Sub is a commercially prepared fast food sandwich available at Subway Singapore outlets. It consists of a 6-inch Italian white bread roll filled with rotisserie-style seasoned chicken breast pieces and standard vegetables including lettuce, tomato, and cucumber, served without sauce. The standard serving weighs approximately 220g and provides approximately 345 kcal, 23g protein, 5g fat (of which ~1g is saturated fat), 48g carbohydrate, and 735mg sodium. The chicken is prepared using a rotisserie-style cooking method, giving it a moist, lightly seasoned flavour without the added fat of deep-frying. The Italian white bread roll is soft and mildly flavoured. This sub is widely regarded as one of the lowest-fat fast food main meals available in Singapore. The glycaemic index is approximately 60 (medium), driven primarily by the Italian white bread component, with a glycaemic load of approximately 28.8 per serving. Clinically, the no-sauce version is notable for its very low saturated fat content (~1g per serving), making it suitable for cardiovascular health. However, the moderate-high sodium content (735mg without sauce) requires attention for individuals managing hypertension. For Type 2 Diabetes management, choosing wholegrain bread (GI ~55) and omitting sauces is recommended. The sub is certified halal and is dairy-free. Sauces significantly alter the nutritional profile: Chipotle Southwest sauce adds approximately 100 kcal and 200mg sodium per serving.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium) are sourced directly from Subway Singapore's official menu nutrition page, which is an approved branded food source. Per-100g values are derived by dividing the declared 220g serving values. Micronutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central comparable entries and are retained from the verified input record. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for soft white wheat bread, which is the primary glycaemic driver. Confidence is set to high because the primary macronutrient data comes from the manufacturer's official nutritional disclosure.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown is not reported in Subway Singapore's official nutritional data and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific commercial product configuration. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not reported by Subway Singapore's official nutritional data. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per Subway Singapore official nutritional data (product formulation). 4. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per Subway Singapore official nutritional data; consistent with USDA FoodData Central values for comparable chicken sub sandwiches. 5. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for soft Italian-style white wheat sub rolls; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific Subway product. GL per serving = (60 × 47.96) / 100 ≈ 28.8. 6. Nutritional profile reflects the no-sauce, standard-vegetable configuration only. Adding sauces (e.g., Chipotle Southwest: +100 kcal, +200mg Na; Garlic Aioli: +170mg Na) significantly changes the profile. 7. Wholegrain bread option reduces GI to approximately 55 and adds approximately 2g dietary fibre per serving — clinically preferred for Type 2 Diabetes management. 8. The sub is certified halal at Subway Singapore outlets. 9. q8 fix (iteration 3): performed verbatim self-check of all aliases_by_language string values against flat aliases array. All entries in aliases_by_language.chinese (including full local_names strings '赛百味烤鸡三明治（6英寸，意式白面包，无酱）' and '賽百味烤雞三明治（6英寸，意式白麵包，無醬）'), aliases_by_language.malay (including 'Sub Ayam Panggang Subway (6 inci, Roti Putih Itali, Tanpa Sos)'), aliases_by_language.tamil (including full Tamil string), aliases_by_language.hindi (including full Hindi string), and aliases_by_language.romanized ('Sàibǎiwèi Kǎo Jī Sānmíngzhì') are now confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Added mandatory local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singapore-market food.

Iteration 1: Restructured health_context to use required template keys (hypertension, type2_diabetes, cardiovascular_disease) with human_condition_name, concern_level, key_factor, and guidance fields; added cardiovascular_disease condition which was absent in input.
Iteration 1: Replaced input health_suitability condition key 'diabetes' with 'type2_diabetes' and human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per scoring model requirement.
Iteration 1: Mapped per_100g fields to RESULT template field names.
Iteration 1: Set monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g to -1 and documented in notes per G3 rule.
Iteration 1: Upgraded gi_source to reference University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021).
Iteration 2: Expanded aliases flat array and aliases_by_language; added full local_names strings to flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Performed exhaustive verbatim self-check of all aliases_by_language string values against flat aliases array. Confirmed every entry in aliases_by_language.chinese, .malay, .tamil, .hindi, and .romanized appears verbatim in flat aliases. Added 'Sàibǎiwèi Kǎo Jī Sānmíngzhì' to flat aliases to cover romanized entry. Flat aliases array now contains all 23 verbatim strings from aliases_by_language. q8 failure from iteration 2 resolved.
